nativeRange.habitat: Riversides and periodically flooded marshy areas in the Amazon basin and other South American wetlands

summary: A vibrant South American stem plant with red to purple leaves; generally considered the showpiece red mid-to-background plant for planted tanks. Bright coloration is best maintained with strong light, CO2, and iron-rich fertilization.
careNotes: Alternanthera reineckii is one of the few Alternanthera species that grows reliably submersed, and the species-level form is the classic 'red temple' commonly sold for planted aquariums. Sources broadly agree on a temperature range around 22–28 °C, pH 6.0–7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH 2–12, kH 2–10). Submersed stem height varies widely with light and CO2: Buce Plant cites roughly 10–30 cm (4–12"), Tropica's 'Pink' cultivar reaches 25–50 cm, and freshwateraquariumplants.com cites 25–50 cm with taller growth under strong light — averaged here to ~10–50 cm. Lighting is the single most important variable for deep red coloration: medium light keeps the plant alive, but bright/high light (>40 PAR) combined with CO2 injection and iron-rich fertilization (UNS Plant Food, Easy Green, or similar) is generally needed for the rich red-to-purple leaf colour the species is sold for. Without CO2 and strong light, leaves tend to fade toward orange/green and lower leaves can drop. The plant prefers a nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or laterite/root tabs) and benefits from both column dosing and root feeding. Avoid drastic parameter swings — sudden changes can trigger leaf melt and stem rot, especially on freshly planted tissue-culture specimens transitioning from emersed to submersed growth. Cross-source disagreement on care level: Buce Plant lists it as 'easy to moderate' (beginner-friendly), Tropica rates it medium difficulty, and Aquarium Co-Op tags it 'advanced' — averaged to 'intermediate' here since reliable red colour realistically requires CO2 plus high light, but the plant itself is forgiving once established. Common trade variants — 'Mini', 'Rosanervig' (pink-veined), 'Scarlet', 'Mini Sunset', 'Cardinalis', and 'Pink' — share most husbandry with the species-level entry; named cultivars may warrant separate entries later if their requirements diverge meaningfully (notably 'Mini' is a true foreground/carpet variant with a much smaller adult size).
breedingNotes: Propagated by nipping off the terminal bud of a mature stem and replanting the cutting in the substrate; this also encourages lateral side-shoot growth on the mother plant. Stem fragments can also be floated until they develop roots and then planted. When grown emersed, the plant produces small flowers but seed propagation is uncommon in the hobby.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used for lighting (medium-high), CO2 (required), growth rate, height range (~10-30 cm), care level (easy-moderate), pH (6.5-7.5), native range, family. Primary product page rather than the Tropica search URL because the Tropica search root returned no usable per-species detail; Tropica's per-cultivar pages were used as supplementary.

additional.name: Tropica
additional.notes: Mini cultivar page — used for cross-checking lighting (medium), CO2 (medium, 6-14 mg/L), growth rate (medium), care difficulty (medium). The Mini variant is a smaller foreground form; Tropica's 'Pink' cultivar (https://tropica.com/en/plants/plantdetails/4436/4436) was also consulted and reports height 20-30+ cm and South American origin.

additional.name: Freshwater Aquarium Plants
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22-28 °C optimal), lighting (moderate-high), CO2 (required), height (25-50 cm), placement (mid-background), propagation (terminal-bud cuttings), and native range (Amazon South America).

additional.name: Aquarium Co-Op
additional.notes: Cross-checked placement (midground/background), lighting (high for deepest red coloration), CO2, and propagation. Notes 'advanced' care level — averaged with Buce/Tropica's easier ratings to 'intermediate'.
